quoteLast: After the bulletin was      issued at 5.30 this evening, the President continued in much      the same condition as during the afternoon, the pulse      varying from 102 to 106, with rather increased force and      volume. After taking nourishment he fell into a quiet sleep      about thirty-five minutes before his death, and while asleep      his pulse rose to 120, and was somewhat more feeble. At ten      minutes after ten o'clock he awoke, complaining of severe      pain over the region of the heart, and almost immediately      became unconscious, and ceased to breathe at 10.35."
